methodology: Cross-referenced each prospect across Kiper Top-25 (ESPN), Jeremiah Top-50 4.0 (NFL.com), Brugler Top-100 (The Athletic), PFF big board, NFL Mock Draft Database consensus, Tankathon, Bleacher Report NFL Scouting Dept, and The Ringer/McShay. 'actual_analyst_range' reflects the min-max observed across tier-1 sources in April 2026.

source_citations.NFLMocks: top landing spots piece implies top-15

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Internet boards (NFLDraftBuzz, B/R) have him top-5, but Kiper left him OUT of his top 25 entirely. Significant disagreement driven by projection risk (LB/EDGE hybrid, modest production vs elite athleticism).
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #16 is actually close to Kiper's implicit rank (~26) and DJ's likely slot (rookie junior, 6.5 sacks). Length-speed profile is elite but LBs rarely go top-5 in modern drafts. Our model is arguably MORE realistic than the aggregated #2.
validations.action: Hold. Consider minor bump to ~#12 by adding 'elite_athleticism' and 'position_versatility' tags, but do NOT chase the #2 aggregate — it's distorted by non-tier-1 boards.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Tight cluster 6-10. Essentially unanimous he is WR1 or WR2. Our #17 has no support from any tier-1 source.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our model likely penalizes contested-catch athlete with modest YAC. But size + hands + 5-year first-round WR streak from Ohio State make #17 unsupportable. Ohio State pipeline bias may be underweighted.
validations.action: Bump ~7-8 slots. Add tags: 'pro_ready', 'WR1_class', 'ohio_state_pipeline', 'contested_catch'. Verify Ohio State WR archetype multiplier.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Actually split. PFF top-15, but many evaluators have slot-size concerns keeping him near #20-25. A to Z Sports explicitly calls the Lemon evaluation 'semi-controversial'.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #24 falls within the observed range (10-25) and matches the more conservative slot-only evaluations. Combine interview concerns also weigh down tier-1 boards. Defensible but slightly too low — consensus centers ~15-17.
validations.action: Moderate bump ~4-6 slots. Add tags: 'biletnikoff_winner', 'elite_route_runner', 'slot_specialist'. Don't overshoot to top-12 — slot size concerns are real.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Very wide range due to medical (ACL rehab, missed 2025). Healthy scouts rank top-10; medically-adjusted boards push to 20s-30s. Kiper #16 is the fair midpoint.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #27 reflects the medical discount aggressively. Per memory note, McCoy medical multiplier is 0.50 and targeting 50-60% R1 landings — current setting may be slightly too punitive. Kiper #16 with full injury info says the midpoint is #15-18.
validations.action: Bump ~7-10 slots. Soften medical penalty from 0.50 to ~0.65, OR add 'elite_man_cover', 'best_tape_CB_class' tags. Target center: #17.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Tight cluster 16-26 across tier-1. DJ aggressive at #16, Kiper #24. Our #33 is below the lowest tier-1 estimate.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: 6-4 safety frame with coverage upside — rare archetype. Our penalty likely from small-school (Toledo) and modest combine (4.52 40). But tape is tier-1 and DJ/Kiper/Miller all have him R1. Our #33 is too low.
validations.action: Bump ~8 slots. Add tags: 'rare_size_safety', 'box_safety_coverage_upside', 'ball_production'. Reduce small-school discount.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: DJ/Kiper have him R1 (17-22); LWOS and others have late R1/early R2. Real split around R1-R2 bubble.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #25 is the median of the split. Defensible — falls between DJ #17 and LWOS #41. Not a clear model error.
validations.action: Minor bump 3-5 slots optional. Add tags: 'elite_PFF_coverage', 'ball_hawk'. Current rank is within defensible range.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Tight range 7-15. Kiper at the bottom of the range (#15) matches our rank exactly. Slight upside to #12 is justifiable.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #15 = Kiper #15 exactly. Short arms (30 7/8) are a real flag. Top-7 boards are optimistic; our rank is defensible.
validations.action: Optional minor bump to #12. Current rank matches Kiper's final big board. No action required.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Universally DT1, but range is #13 (Kiper pre-combine) to #28 (late mocks). NO analyst has him top-10. 6'3 300 is undersized for elite DT.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #6 is wildly out of range. Even the most optimistic Kiper rank (#13) is 7 slots below ours. Undersized DT without elite pass-rush production — top-10 is unsupportable.
validations.action: DROP ~13 slots to ~#18. Investigate position multiplier for DT — model is over-rewarding run-defense DTs. Remove any 'elite_pass_rusher' tag if present.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Tight cluster 21-35. Stock fell from preseason top-10 to late R1/early R2. No tier-1 source has him top-20.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #14 is 7+ slots above the HIGHEST tier-1 rank (Schrager #21). Parker's stock collapsed due to disappointing 2025. Model is likely anchoring to preseason hype.
validations.action: DROP ~10 slots to ~#24. Remove preseason hype tags. Add 'stock_down_2025', 'limited_bend', 'power_rusher_only'.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Wide range 13-30 — YAC traits have some evaluators excited (NFL.com #13) but most have him #20-30. CBS middle ground at #17.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #9 is above EVERY tier-1 rank. Even NFL.com's optimistic #13 is 4 slots below us. Cooper is a YAC weapon but not a top-10 prospect. Model likely over-weighting YAC/drop-rate metrics.
validations.action: DROP ~8-10 slots to ~#18. Audit YAC multiplier. Add 'size_concerns' or 'not_X_receiver' tag.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Huge split. B/R has him EDGE1 overall, but DJ/Kiper have him #23-32. Elite size (6'6, 276, 34 3/8 arms) vs production-talent gap.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #8 aligns with B/R's elite-ceiling view but ignores that production didn't match talent in 2025. Kiper/DJ at #23-32 are more credible. However, our #8 is defensible on pure tools and elite-ceiling archetype.
validations.action: DROP ~8-10 slots to ~#17. Keep 'elite_size', 'rare_length' tags but add 'production_gap_2025'.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Highly split — 'mystery box' prospect. PFN has him #17, DJ has him #35. Ceiling-vs-floor is the cleanest single-prospect divergence in the class.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #19 falls at the top end of the plausible range and matches PFN's #17. Defensible for a ceiling-bet, but risky — DJ at #35 suggests floor is R2. Within range.
validations.action: Optional drop ~3-5 slots to ~#23. Our #19 is aggressive but not indefensible given the split. Add 'boom_bust', 'developmental_ceiling' tags for honesty.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Tight cluster 12-22. Kiper top-12, others 13-17. Elite athletic TE with first-round grade.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #10 is 2 slots above Kiper's #12 — basically within model error. Elite combine performance (93 combine score, #1 TE) supports aggression. Very close to consensus top.
validations.action: Optional drop 2-3 slots to #12-13 to match Kiper exactly. Our rank is essentially defensible.

validations.analyst_disagreement_note: Wide 13-35 range. Redshirt soph with 9.88 RAS but development-stage technique.
validations.trait_justification_for_ours: Our #21 is the midpoint of the plausible range. Defensible. Consensus center is ~#23-26.
validations.action: No action required. Optional drop 2-3 slots. Current rank aligns with the median of tier-1 projections.

top_5_priority_fixes.issue: Our #6 unsupported by any tier-1 source (range #13-28). Undersized DT.
top_5_priority_fixes.action: DROP ~13 slots to #18. Audit DT position multiplier — likely over-rewarding run-defense interior DL.

top_5_priority_fixes.issue: Our #14 is 7 slots above highest tier-1 rank (Schrager #21). Stock collapsed 2025.
top_5_priority_fixes.action: DROP ~10 slots to #24. Remove preseason-hype anchoring. Add stock_down_2025 tag.

top_5_priority_fixes.issue: Our #9 above every tier-1 rank. YAC metric over-weighted.
top_5_priority_fixes.action: DROP ~8 slots to #18. Audit YAC multiplier; add size_concerns tag.

top_5_priority_fixes.issue: Our #8 aligns only with B/R; Kiper/DJ have him #23-32. Production-talent gap.
top_5_priority_fixes.action: DROP ~9 slots to #17. Add production_gap_2025 tag while keeping elite_size.

top_5_priority_fixes.issue: Our #17 has no support — every tier-1 source has him top-10 WR1/WR2.
top_5_priority_fixes.action: BUMP ~8 slots to #9. Add pro_ready, WR1_class, ohio_state_pipeline tags. Verify Ohio State WR archetype.

secondary_fixes.action: Bump ~8 slots (from #27 to ~#18). Soften medical multiplier from 0.50 to ~0.65.
Bump ~8 slots (from #33 to ~#24). Reduce small-school (Toledo) discount.

defensible_as_is: Arvell Reese (#16 — Kiper omitted from top 25; aggregate #2 is inflated by non-tier-1 boards)
Dillon Thieneman (#25 at median of R1/R2 split)
Max Iheanachor (#19 matches PFN's aggressive projection; mystery-box archetype)
Kenyon Sadiq (#10 is 2 slots above Kiper #12; within error)
Caleb Lomu (#21 at median of 13-35 range)
Makai Lemon (partially — some adjustment warranted toward #15-18)
